column2: raijū (雷獣, らいじゅう, lit. "thunder animal/beast")
Raijin (雷神, lit. "Thunder God"), also known as Kaminari-sama (雷様), Raiden-sama (雷電様), Narukami (鳴る神), Raikou (雷公), and Kamowakeikazuchi-no-kami (Raiju is companion)
column7: Dumuzid or Dumuzi or Tammuz (Agriculture, Sheperd, goes to underworld)

column2: Yomi or Yomi-no-kuni (黄泉, 黄泉の国, or 黄泉ノ国)
column3: Diyu (simplified Chinese: 地狱; traditional Chinese: 地獄; pinyin: dìyù; lit. 'earth prison') is the realm of the dead or "hell" in Chinese mythology. ruled by a series of judges (collectively known as the Ten Yama Kings)
